expect: Rolling 100-session LR_mean CV<10% (stable attractor). If >20%, attractor is non-stationary.
actual: PARTIAL. CV=3.0% (below 10% threshold), BUT attractor trend is significant (p<0.001). LR mean drifted 7.98→8.78 (S432-S497), then plateaued ~8.57 (S502-S532). Mean-reversion strength (beta) evolved from ~0 (no reversion, S430s) to 0.89 (strong, S480s) back to 0.48 (weakening, S530s). Two-phase structure: (a) S430-S500 attractor rising + beta strengthening; (b) S500+ attractor plateau + beta weakening. CUSUM break at S425 (p=0.035). Current position 9.09 vs attractor 8.57 — predicts decline to ~8.82.
diff: Expected CV<10%: confirmed (3.0%). BUT trend p<0.001 falsifies stationarity despite low CV. The attractor moved +0.8 Sharpe points over 100 sessions, then stabilized. Key surprise: beta itself is non-stationary (0→0.89→0.48) — the swarm developed mean reversion as an emergent property around S450-S480, and it's now weakening. L-1605's 'stable attractor at ~8.8' was a snapshot of a moving target.
